title: Microcycas calocoma
taxonomy: Microcycas calocoma is classified as a species within the family Cycadeaceae and is considered to be a relic of a group of ancient plants known as cycads. The genus Microcycas is thought to be the sole living representative of the family. As a member of the class Cycadopsida, Microcycas calocoma is part of a group that is characterized by its cone-bearing, seed-producing habit. Within the class Cycadopsida, Microcycas calocoma is placed in the order Cycadales. This classification reflects the unique characteristics and evolutionary history of the Microcycas calocoma species.
uses: Microcycas calocoma is primarily found in the Philippines and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The plant's leaves and seeds are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential anti-cancer and antioxidant properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Microcycas calocoma.

intro: Microcycas calocoma, commonly known as the Dahlish tree or Cycad palm, is a species of plant that belongs to the family Stangeriaceae and is endemic to the Philippines. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, and its extracts have been shown to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, prevent infections, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Microcycas calocoma include cycasin, a toxic compound, as well as other non-toxic compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
